channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Parking, 24th April
item.description:
Van parked on pavement blocking pedestrian access completely FE18WBT Been parked since 8am now 11.40 Totally blocking pavement. Pedestrians forced to walk in street to pass

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Meadway, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous materials & Vehicle repairs on Pavement, 24th April
item.description:
Number 56 uses the pavement to store large pieces of metal and sharp objects. They are also using the pavement to repair vehicles, there is a large trailer being painted orange with the wheels removed on bricks. There is a white van on bricks blocking the pathway to the school. They also have large wheelie bins on the pavement. Not only is this an eyesore but an absolute danger to passing children going to / from the primary school.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Sandpiper Way, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping behind school again. Mixed waste, 24th April
item.description:
Residents advise this waste is being brought here by residents vans of mangold way. Is it possible to get cameras here?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kale Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Debris dumped blocking access, 24th April
item.description:
Yet again more debris fly tipped on Durlston road blocking access

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Durlston Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Constant dumping on balcony of 31-38 East Surrey Grove, 24th April
item.description:
Rubbish dumped daily by lift on balcony of 31-38 Please put up sign or send letters to remind tenants of their responsibilities, this is almost a daily occurrence for several years now

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, East Surrey Grove, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Apparent animal corpse in black bin bag, 24th April
item.description:
A black bin bag has been left here, on the left of the path that branches off Deal Porters Walk and goes down to Clipper Close etc. It smells very bad and is covered in flies and is in all likelihood an abandoned non-human animal corpse. It could be a fox, a dog or a cat, couldn't say from the small part of the bag that was opened. If it is a dog or a cat, please try and identify it so the humans who cared for it may know what happened to it. Thank your for your help, this is an unpleasant one.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 5, Ironside Close, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubish bins not collected, 24th April
item.description:
Black rubbish bins were not collected from 61 Navarino road, please collect :) It’s the third time it happens?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Navarino Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken glass and other debis, 24th April
item.description:
Last Saturday the fire brigade was called to a location 1 to 6 Ladywell House, Longbury Drive and as a result there is broken glass and other Debris in the road as a result of a fire in a car I believe. This has left broken glass and other Debris which is a hazard to people and car tyres
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 52, Longbury Drive, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road Bollard Southall Place - Trip Hazard, 24th April
item.description:
Bollard on Southall Place appears to have been hit by van. Unable to make safe. When attempting to make safe by lifting up, it brings up the entire base of the bollard, creating a further trip hazard. Bollard needs to be replaced. It is on public land, it does not form part of the private Empire Square estate.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sterry Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Branch has fallen from tree, 24th April
item.description:
I live at 49 Cranmore Road, BR7 6EP and park my car outside my house. Due to the recent windy weather a 5ft branch has fallen from the tree (see photos) Fortunately my car was not parked under the tree at the time. Can the tree be removed to prevent this happening in the future. Thanks, Robert Bunce 0794 1177179

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Walden Avenue, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped grey bag, 24th April
item.description:
Flytipped grey bag
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sydenham Hill, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: This car has been parked here for a long time., 24th April
item.description:
This car has been parked up in this spot for a long time. It even has mould inside it and has what looks like junk. It’s taking up a valuable parking space .

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Anerley Park, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Japanese knotweed growing, 24th April
item.description:
I spotted early stages of Japanese knotweed growing wild just off the path of the greenchain walk way between Bluebell close/Sydenham hill and Talisman square/highlevel drive. Lewisham council and environmental agency have both been contacted but sadly both dismissed this issue and tried to pass me off to each other. My home and many other properties back onto this wooded area. If this invasive plant is not taken care of soon, it will spread like wildfire and many properties are at risk of serious damage. What.three.words location is Hello.cycle.visual. Please help

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): High Level Drive, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Corner of carlisle and chevening, 24th April
item.description:
Rubbish all over stree

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Carlisle Road, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Manhole Cover Missing - Needs Urgent Attention, 24th April
item.description:
There is a missing manhole cover in the street in front of 51 Tanner Street. There is a hole at least a meter deep. It presents an immediate danger to motorists, cyclists and pedestrians.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 51, Tanner Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Corner of Buckley Road and Kilburn High Road outside James Crook Funeral Directors, 24th April
item.description:
Huge fly tip of construction and domestic waste including mattresses and dismantled furniture

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kilburn High Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load

item.title: Grass and bushes need cutting in dog area, 24th April
item.description:
Theres stinging nettles growing thats bad for the dogs, my dogs been hurt by the massive hole in the grass and the thorns off the rose bush.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Inville Road, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Layout of the yellow box at this junction is risky and impractical to navigate safely. A review of the road layout requested., 24th April
item.description:
Layout of the yellow box at this junction is risky and impractical to navigate safely. A review of the road layout requested.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Green Lane, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Repeated mess at this charity clothes point, 24th April
item.description:
This charity clothes bank is always a mess. Clothes and rubbish always around. This is unacceptable—we want a clean Hackney!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Dalston Lane, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Garden furniture dumped on corner of Montbretia Close, 24th April
item.description:
Only just been dumped there

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Okemore Gardens, St Mary Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
